summary: Plan-only remediation assessment for five open PRs. No merge is recommended because the artifact lacks complete merge preflight proof, especially Codex /review and hydrated comment/review-bot resolution. One narrow repair path is concrete for #93113; the larger automation PR #91089 needs maintainer judgment because of broad CI automation scope and failing checks.

actions.reason: Likely mergeable after full review preflight, but the required comment and Codex /review proof is missing from the live artifact.
Docs change is small, but low-signal-docs labeling plus missing merge preflight proof makes this a maintainer decision, not an automated merge.
Broad CI automation change with failing checks requires maintainer judgment before any repair or replacement plan is safe.
Dependency bump appears narrow and checks pass, but automated merge cannot be recommended without the required review preflight.
Repair the contributor branch narrowly, preserve AgentArcLab credit, rerun the memory-core and changed-file validation, then obtain Codex /review before any merge recommendation.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93113 on the contributor branch by addressing the failing memory-core lint/bundled checks without broadening the patch, then rerun focused validation and Codex /review before any merge recommendation.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(memory-core): repair dreaming phase status reporting
repair_candidate.pr_body:
Repairs source PR #93113 while preserving AgentArcLab's attribution.

Summary:
- keep the phase-aware memory status behavior from #93113
- address the failing lint/bundled checks narrowly in memory-core files
- preserve the focused regression coverage for light-only, REM-only, and deep-detail status output

Validation:
- pnpm check:changed
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts
- pnpm lint -- extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts

Source PR credit: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93113 by AgentArcLab.
credit_notes: Preserve contributor credit for AgentArcLab as the source PR author.
